events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need parse scenario. Task: identify component/condition and visible defect cues. Setting heavy industrial, VFDs & motor controls, rough-complete. Observed field condition: missing load reactor on long motor leads. Component condition categories may apply: installed-defective. Need decision fail because non-compliant? Missing load reactor on long motor leads can cause reflected wave voltage spikes, motor insulation damage, bearing currents. NEC 430 requires installation per manufacturer instructions; manufacturer manuals may require load reactors for long cable runs. So fail. Risk high? Could be high due to equipment damage, safety, production loss. Maybe medium? Missing reactor is a design/install defect that can damage motor/VFD, but not immediate shock/fire? However high in industrial context. Let's decide high. work_stage rough-complete. component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 80? Rough-complete. value 0. sound_source irrelevant. confidence 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty. findings: missing load reactor on long motor leads; VFD output not protected against reflected wave/ dv/dt stress; non-compliance with manufacturer installation requirements. actions: install specified load reactor/dv/dt filter at VFD output per drawings and manufacturer manual; verify cable length and reactor rating; inspect motor/VFD for damage; update as-builts. rationale: Long motor leads between VFD and motor require output/load reactor to limit voltage spikes and dv/dt; absence violates NEC 430 (follow manufacturer instructions) and manufacturer manuals, risking motor insulation failure. references: NEC 430.6(A), 430.122? Actually NEC 430.122 is motor circuit conductors. NEC 110.3(B) installation and use per listing. Manufacturer VFD manual installation requirements. Let's include NEC 430.6(A), 110.3(B), manufacturer manuals. Need valid JSON only. Ensure no prose outside.

A load reactor is required by the manufacturer manual and engineered design; its absence makes the installation non-compliant with NEC 430 and manufacturer instructions, so the work fails rough-complete inspection.",
